semanticCore: Shariah, religion, dictation, boredom, or hot ashes
quranicRelevance: Creed/religion of Abraham (Millah - 'millata ibrahima hanifan'), and dictating a document (Imla' - 'falyumlil allathi 'alayhi al-haq').

keyDerivations.meaning: Religion, creed, code of law, or community path
To dictate (a text), write out, or prolong/give time

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَلْيُمْلِلِ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الحَقُّ (2:282): And let the one who owes the debt dictate (the contract detail).
أُمْلِي لَهُمْ (68:45): I grant them time/prolong their duration.
discardedNoise: Boredom or weariness (ملل) in human emotion.
summaryEn: The root represents religion and dictation, undergirding the legacy of monotheistic creed (Millah) and transaction contract dictation (Imlal).
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الملة والإملاء، مؤصلاً للمنهج العقدي التوحيدي (ملة إبراهيم) وإملاء وكتابة العقود التجارية والمالية (يملل).
rawText: أَمْلَلْتُ 4 & أَمْلَيْتُ: see 4 in art. حظ. === أَمَلَّ: see an ex. voce أَدَلَّ. اِمْتَلَّ مِلَّتَهُ 8 He follows his way of religion: see 8 in art. شرع. مَلاَلٌ : see 1. مَلُولٌ Conceiving [frequent] disgust. (Msb.) See ذَوَّاقٌ. مَلِيلٌ A man burned by the sun; as also 🡻مَمْلُولٌ. (TA.) See an ex. in a verse cited voce أَصْرَمُ; and see طُلْمَهٌ. مَلَّةٌ The hollow that is made for baking bread: or the hot dust and ashes [in which the bread is baked]. (Msb.) === Hot ashes: (S, K:) ashes, and earth, in which fire is kindled. (TA, art. خبز.) === خُبْزُ مَلَّةٍ Bread baked in hot ashes. (S.) [It is generally made in the form of thick round cakes.] مَلَّهُ 1 He put it (namely bread, or flesh-meat,) into hot ashes, [to bake, or roast]. (K, &c.) === مَلَّ الثَّوْبَ He sewed, or tacked, the garment, or piece of cloth, [slightly,] previously to the [stronger] sewing termed الكَفّ. (S.) See also شَلَّ. === مَلِلْتُهُ, & مَلِلْتُ عَنْهُ, I was avers from it; (T;) loathed it; was disgusted by it, with it, or at it; (T, S, K;) [was weary of it;] turned away from it with disgust. (T.) See سَئِمَ. === مَلَّ مَلاَلُكَ [May thy disgust pass away, or cease]: see أَضَلَّ ٱللّٰهُ ضَلَالَكَ. مَمْلُولٌ Flesh-meat covered over in live coals. (TA, art. عرض.) === See مَلِيلٌ. مُلْمُولٌ An iron style with which one writes on tablets. (K.) === The style, or bodkin, with which collyrium is applied to the eyes. (S, K.) In the CK, incorrectly, مَلْمُولٌ: the former is found in MS. copies of the K, as well as in the S, and is right accord. to the TK. مِلَّةٌ A religion; (S, Msb, K;) a way of belief and practice in respect of religion. (T, &c.) === See 8.
